opp.name: Associated Materials [S2P] March'2026
opp.next_step: June 16 2026 - Wendy: Continue market review over the next 12–18 months and reach out to Edward directly if you’d like to explore a deeper discussion or tailored demo
opp.ais_why:
• Wendy Rocco sat through March 2026 demo of Merlin Intake chatbot in Teams, Anna autonomous tail-spend negotiation, and AI-driven supplier onboarding. But she cut Edward off mid-pitch saying 'I really wanna understand more of the functionality' rather than the AI story.
• Her takeaway: 'continue market review over the next 12-18 months' and contact Ed directly if she wants a tailored demo. Jaggaer contract has 18 months left so no urgency.
• Polite curiosity, demo absorbed, but not buying the agentic vision yet - she's exploring the broader market. Score reflects exposure to all AI cues with low conversion signal.

sources: Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'End users bypass system causing maverick spend and loss of contracted pricing'
Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'I have an idea' (tail spend size, when asked, but did not quantify)
metrics.narrative: Buyer articulated operational pain but did NOT quantify financial impact. On the 2026-03-06 intro call, Wendy Rocco (Director of Procurement: Indirect) stated: 'End users bypass system causing maverick spend and loss of contracted pricing' (per Avoma note). The pain is real and supplier-confirmed: low adoption of the current Jaggaer system drives off-contract buying. However, Wendy provided no dollar figure for tail-spend leakage, no headcount cost of low-self-service, and no cycle-time or savings target. When asked 'Do you know how big your tail spend is?' she replied 'I have an idea' but did not share it. The business problem is confirmed; quantifying it into a measurable ROI case remains an open deliverable and should be prioritized in the next substantive conversation to anchor the value narrative and justify budget.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Wendy Rocco (Director of Procurement: Indirect), only buyer contact role

Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'if in the event I need to get back on in touch with you, which is probably more than likely, after I do a few more looks out there... Would I get ahold of you, Ed?'
Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'Where do you think that your tool sets you apart from the marketplace?' (engaged, qualifying tone)
Next Step 2026-06-16: rep-initiated soft touch, no buyer response yet (passive engagement post-intro)
champion.narrative: Wendy Rocco (Director of Procurement: Indirect) is a developing champion with strong category ownership but limited EB/DM access visibility at this stage. She initiated the intro call on 2026-03-06, engaged actively for the full 30 minutes, asked sharp qualifying questions ('Where do you think that your tool sets you apart from the marketplace?', 'Are you only a high end market solution?'), and stated clear intent to re-engage when her evaluation timeline advances ('if in the event I need to get back on in touch with you, which is probably more than likely, after I do a few more looks out there'). She owns the indirect procurement function and the pain (maverick spend, low adoption), positioning her as the natural internal advocate. However, her access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker (likely her VP or the CFO) has not been demonstrated — no introductions offered, no exec escalation discussed, no internal steering-committee mention. Her engagement is currently PASSIVE post-intro: she set the next step as 'reach out to Edward directly if you'd like to explore', putting the onus on her to re-initiate; since 2026-03-06 (112 days ago), she has not circled back. The rep soft-touch on 2026-06-16 (10 days ago) has not yet drawn a response. Champion strength will develop when (1) she re-engages with a formal RFI/demo request, (2) she introduces other stakeholders (her VP, procurement peers, IT/InfoSec), and (3) she advocates for Zycus in a multi-vendor eval. Until then, she is a qualified contact, not yet an active champion.

sources: Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Zycus rep: 'You're probably using Jagger' → Wendy Rocco: 'Yeah. I think I told Rishab that.'
Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'We're still under contract for, like, another year and a half.'
Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Zycus rep: 'this is something that Jagger doesn't even have' (re: Merlin Intake)
No other competitor named in any Salesforce field or Avoma call
competition.narrative: Jaggaer is the incumbent and the sole named competitor; no other vendors have surfaced. On the 2026-03-06 intro call, Wendy Rocco confirmed: 'We are currently utilizing a tool right now' and the Zycus rep stated 'You're probably using Jagger' to which Wendy replied 'Yeah. I think I told Rishab that.' Wendy's Jaggaer contract expires in approximately 18 months from March 2026 (around September 2027), and she is conducting an early market scan to evaluate alternatives before renewal. She stated: 'right now is the time that I really wanna start just exploring what else is out there. Does it make sense for what we are using, or like, do we have the right functionality? What else is available in the marketplace?' This positions Jaggaer as the baseline/incumbent to beat, not an active rival in a multi-vendor bake-off (yet). Wendy has not named any other vendor (Coupa, Ariba, GEP, etc.) on the call or in any Salesforce field. The competitive wedge is clear and was articulated on the intro call: (1) Merlin Intake (MS Teams chatbot for end-user ease of use) — Zycus rep stated 'this is something that Jagger doesn't even have'; (2) Agentic AI for touchless invoices, tail-spend negotiation, and supplier onboarding — capabilities Jaggaer lacks. Wendy showed interest in both. The risk is that Wendy's 12–18 month timeline leaves room for other vendors to enter her shortlist during her broader market scan; Zycus must stay top-of-mind and position the AI differentiation as the wedge when she re-engages in late 2026.

sources: Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'End users bypass system causing maverick spend and loss of contracted pricing'

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer clearly articulated operational pain tied to low adoption and maverick spend, owned by Wendy Rocco (Director of Procurement: Indirect). On the 2026-03-06 intro call, Wendy stated: 'End users bypass system causing maverick spend and loss of contracted pricing' (per Avoma note). The pain is structural: the current Jaggaer system is too complex for occasional/end users, driving them to either bypass procurement entirely (maverick spend, off-contract buying) or call Wendy's lean team for support ('low self service'), which does not scale. Zycus's Avoma note captured this broader context: '90% of rollouts fail due to low user adoption or complicated processes.' Wendy showed particular interest in the Merlin Intake MS Teams chatbot as a potential solution ('Do you use MS Teams, Wendy, at you do?' → 'Yeah. We use it for calls. We use it for meetings. We use it for chat.'), signaling that ease of use and end-user adoption are her top pain points. The pain is confirmed and owned; quantifying its financial impact (tail-spend leakage, procurement team overhead, cycle-time cost) is the next step.

sources: Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco: 'We Docusign it and route it out through Jagger' (NDA workflow mentioned, but no S2P contracting process discussed)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, InfoSec, and implementation scoping have not been discussed. The 2026-03-06 intro call was purely functional discovery; no mention of MSA/SOW templates, procurement channel (direct vs partner), legal review cycle, or InfoSec requirements. Wendy did reference an NDA workflow ('We Docusign it and route it out through Jaggaer'), indicating some baseline contracting infrastructure exists, but the process for a platform replacement contract (redlines, approvals, partner involvement, ERP integration scope) is unknown. For a $350k S2P SaaS deal with Merlin/Agentic AI modules, expect InfoSec and data-privacy review, possible SI scoping if implementation services are needed, and a multi-month legal cycle. Current_Contract_Expiration__c (the Jaggaer contract end, approximately September 2027) is the natural forcing function; contracting mechanics will surface when Wendy moves to vendor selection and RFP response in late 2026 or early 2027. Until then, this is a known gap.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic buyer is not identified. The intro call on 2026-03-06 was entirely with Wendy Rocco, Director of Procurement: Indirect, who owns the category and is the user/evaluator but not the budget authority. No CFO, VP Finance, or C-level executive has appeared on any call, in any contact role, or in any Salesforce field. Wendy reports to a VP (who manages the direct side), but that VP has not been named, introduced, or engaged. The Salesforce synthesis-map fields for EB (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) are unknown/not read this sweep. For a $350k S2P platform replacement at a $1.3B revenue company, budget sign-off will require at least VP-level or CFO approval. The next milestone — when the buyer moves from market scan to vendor selection in late 2026 or early 2027 — must surface and engage the budget holder; until then, this is a known gap that will block any forecast upgrade.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is implied by the buyer's stated timeline but not yet formalized. Wendy Rocco stated on 2026-03-06: 'We are currently utilizing a tool right now. We're still under contract for, like, another year and a half. So right now is the time that I really wanna start just exploring what else is out there.' This places the Jaggaer contract expiration around September 2027 (18 months from March 2026) and positions the current activity as early market scan, not active procurement. Wendy is running a solo discovery at this stage — no committee, no RFI/RFP process, no multi-vendor bake-off yet. She asked about modularity ('Are they ad hoc, or do they all come together... is there a way to right size the need?') and company fit ('Are you only a high end market solution? Are you a middle market solution?'), indicating she is qualifying vendors before advancing. The approval chain is unknown: Wendy reports to a VP (direct materials), but whether that VP, a CFO, or a procurement committee will approve the final decision has not been discussed. The next process milestone is Wendy circling back in late 2026 or early 2027 to request deeper demos, RFI responses, or a formal eval; the current Next Step ('reach out to Edward directly if you'd like to explore a deeper discussion or tailored demo') puts the ball in her court. Visibility into the formal process and approval gates is a gap that must close when she re-engages.

decision_criteria.narrative: Buyer stated high-level evaluation priorities on the 2026-03-06 intro call but has NOT run a formal RFI/RFP or published detailed decision criteria yet. Wendy Rocco asked 'Where do you think that your tool sets you apart from the marketplace?' and expressed interest in understanding 'functionally, what does your tool do?' and 'what else is available in the marketplace'. She confirmed she is comparing Zycus to the current Jaggaer solution and will evaluate whether she has 'the right functionality'. The pain points that will drive criteria are documented: (1) low end-user adoption and complexity (Avoma note: '90% of rollouts fail due to low user adoption or complicated processes'), (2) maverick spend / off-contract buying, and (3) ease of use (Wendy showed interest in the Merlin Intake MS Teams chatbot as a potential adoption accelerator). However, no structured scorecard, weighted criteria, or use-case test matrix has been shared. The decision criteria will crystallize when Wendy moves from discovery to formal RFI/shortlist phase in late 2026; capturing and shaping those criteria early — especially around AI-driven adoption and agentic automation — is the strategic play.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Buyer is AI Curious with clear operational appetite for AI-driven adoption and automation. Wendy showed interest in Merlin Intake (MS Teams chatbot) and Agentic AI capabilities (touchless invoices, tail-spend negotiation) on the intro call, and these capabilities directly address her stated pain (low adoption, maverick spend, complexity). She did not express skepticism or resistance; her questions were pragmatic and qualifying ('Where do you think that your tool sets you apart?', 'Are you only a high end market solution?'). However, she did not articulate a proactive AI strategy or demand AI-first capabilities unprompted — the AI interest was responsive to Zycus's positioning. The Products field includes 'Merlin', 'Merlin Intake', and 'Agentic AI', indicating the opp scope already reflects AI modules. No AIS score/status/why is populated in Salesforce. The signal is AI Curious (interested and receptive when shown the value, but not yet AI Hungry with an explicit AI mandate).

Single-threaded: only one buyer contact (Wendy Rocco) is engaged. No multi-threading to IT, finance, procurement peers, or Wendy's VP. Champion dependency risk: if Wendy leaves, de-prioritizes, or selects another vendor during her broader market scan, Zycus has no other advocate or relationship.

items.action: Send a value-reinforcement touchpoint with a tailored Merlin Intake use-case one-pager to Wendy Rocco.

items.expected_effect: Re-engage Wendy with a concrete, pain-targeted value artifact (e.g. a one-pager: 'How Merlin Intake reduces maverick spend and boosts end-user adoption at mid-market manufacturers') that keeps Zycus top-of-mind during her 12–18 month market scan and tees up her next demo request when her timeline advances in late 2026. A passive 'checking in' email risks no reply; a value-add artifact with a clear use-case tied to her stated pain (low adoption, maverick spend) gives her a reason to respond and share internally.

items.action: Develop a quantified tail-spend savings model for Associated Materials and share it with Wendy as a preview of Agentic AI ROI.
items.trigger: Wendy stated pain (maverick spend, tail-spend leakage) on 2026-03-06 intro call but did not quantify. When asked 'Do you know how big your tail spend is?' she replied 'I have an idea' but did not share a number. No quantified ROI case exists yet.
items.expected_effect: Build a preliminary tail-spend savings model (e.g. benchmark: mid-market manufacturers typically have 15–25% of spend in tail categories; Agentic AI negotiation agent delivers 8–12% savings on tail spend) and share it with Wendy as a preview of the ROI the Agentic AI negotiation agent can deliver. This anchors the value narrative early, differentiates Zycus from Jaggaer (which lacks this capability), and positions the ROI case as a reason to prioritize Zycus when she runs her formal RFI/RFP in late 2026. A quantified ROI case is also a prerequisite for engaging the Economic Buyer (CFO, VP Finance) when Wendy re-initiates.

items.action: Request an intro to the relevant stakeholder or the CFO to align on procurement modernization priorities ahead of the Jaggaer contract renewal.
items.trigger: No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker has been identified or engaged. Wendy reports to a VP (direct materials) but that VP is unnamed and unengaged. For a $350k S2P platform replacement, EB sign-off will be required.
items.expected_effect: Surface and engage the Economic Buyer (likely Wendy's VP or the CFO) to validate budget appetite, align on procurement modernization priorities (AI-driven adoption, maverick-spend reduction, tail-spend savings), and de-risk the deal by multi-threading to power. Frame the request to Wendy as 'We'd love to align with your VP on procurement strategy ahead of your Jaggaer renewal decision — would you be open to a brief intro when your timeline advances?' Position it as a value-add strategic conversation, not a sales push. EB engagement is a gate for any forecast upgrade and reduces single-thread champion dependency risk.

items.action: Add the missing buyer-side contact roles to the opportunity to reflect multi-thread progress.
items.trigger: Only 1 buyer-side contact role on this opp (Wendy Rocco), below the multi-thread bar. Account-level contacts (Jacob Burroughs Controller, Powell Anthony CFO, James Cho Director of Supply Chain, Sharyn Price Category Manager, others) exist but are not linked to this opp.
items.expected_effect: Improve stakeholder visibility and multi-thread tracking. Identify which account-level contacts (e.g. Jacob Burroughs Controller, Powell Anthony CFO, James Cho Director of Supply Chain) are relevant to this S2P eval and add them as contact roles once Wendy introduces them or they appear on a call. This is an action item, not housekeeping: multi-threading beyond Wendy is a strategic priority to de-risk champion dependency and gain access to the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker.

items.action: Prepare a live Merlin Intake MS Teams demo environment for when Wendy requests a deeper demo in late 2026.

She stated she will 'reach out to Edward directly if you'd like to explore a deeper discussion or tailored demo' when her evaluation timeline advances.
items.expected_effect: Pre-build a live, tailored Merlin Intake demo environment (e.g. end-user requesting an NDA or ordering indirect materials via the relevant stakeholder chatbot, integrated with a mock Associated Materials procurement workflow) so when Wendy re-engages for a deeper demo in late 2026 or early 2027, Zycus can deliver a best-in-class, use-case-driven proof of the AI differentiation. A live demo beats a slide deck and accelerates her conviction that Zycus solves her adoption problem better than Jaggaer or any other competitor.

items: Positive: Strong product-pain fit confirmed on intro call (Merlin Intake MS Teams chatbot and Agentic AI directly address Wendy's stated pain: low adoption, maverick spend, complexity). Wendy engaged actively, asked sharp qualifying questions, and stated intent to re-engage.

Positive: AI differentiation clearly articulated and validated as a wedge vs Jaggaer incumbent ('Jagger doesn't even have' Merlin Intake, per Zycus rep). Products field includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, and Agentic AI modules.

Neutral: Buyer explicitly stated a 12–18 month evaluation timeline; next substantive milestone (RFI/demo request) not expected until late 2026 or early 2027. Current posture (early market scan, awaiting buyer re-initiation) is consistent with stage and timeline.
Negative: Single-threaded (only Wendy Rocco engaged; no VP, CFO, IT, or procurement peer introduced). Champion dependency risk.
Negative: No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified or engaged. Gate-blocker for forecast upgrade.

Negative: Passive engagement post-intro: no buyer re-initiation in 112 days since intro call. Rep soft-touch on 2026-06-16 (10 days ago) has not yet drawn a response. Risk that she evaluates other vendors during her market scan without Zycus staying top-of-mind.
confidence_signals.summary: Mixed confidence: strong product-pain fit and clear AI differentiation, but thin engagement (one intro call 112 days ago, no buyer re-initiation since), single-threaded (only Wendy Rocco), no EB/DM identified, and a long buyer timeline (12–18 months) that leaves room for competitive displacement. The deal is live and on track for its stage, but forward momentum is buyer-dependent and currently stalled awaiting her re-engagement.

north_star_verdict.math: 162 days to close (2026-12-05). Created 2026-03-09 (109 days ago), in Qualified stage. Introductory call 2026-03-06 (112 days ago) established the evaluation scope; Jaggaer contract expires in approximately 18 months from March 2026 (around September 2027). Buyer explicitly stated she is conducting market review now for a decision 12–18 months out. Rep reached out 2026-06-16 (10 days ago) with a soft-touch nurture message; no buyer response yet. LastActivityDate 2026-06-24 (2 days ago) indicates recent logged rep activity. No close-date slip; stage aligns with the buyer's early exploration posture. Pace is consistent with a long enterprise evaluation cycle where the next substantive milestone is 6+ months away.

Qualified stage + Pipeline forecast + 12–18 month buyer timeline + $350k amount = the deal is live but very early; the forecast category (Pipeline) is honest for this stage, but the December 2026 close date is a placeholder inconsistent with the buyer's stated 18-month review window (which extends into mid-2027 at earliest).
north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage qualified deal aligned with buyer's 18-month timeline to September 2027 Jaggaer contract expiry, but stalled since March intro call; rep's June nurture outreach went unanswered, signaling weak buyer engagement despite clear competitive wedge and stated market review intent.

flags: Economic Buyer unknown: no CFO, VP Finance, or C-level budget authority has been identified or engaged. For a $350k S2P platform replacement, EB sign-off will be required. Map and engage the EB when Wendy re-initiates in late 2026; this is a gate-blocker for any forecast upgrade to Best Case or Commit.
best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage qualified deal with strong product-pain fit but thin stakeholder coverage and no EB/DM engagement yet. The buyer is in a long evaluation cycle (12–18 months); the strategic play is to stay top-of-mind via light nurture, quantify the pain into an ROI case when she re-engages, and multi-thread to power (VP, CFO) before the formal RFP phase in late 2026 or early 2027.

competitive_position.summary: Jaggaer is the sole competitor and the incumbent to displace. Wendy Rocco's contract with Jaggaer expires in approximately 18 months from March 2026 (around September 2027), and she is conducting an early market scan now to evaluate alternatives before renewal. No other vendor has been named or implied. The competitive wedge is clear and was validated on the 2026-03-06 intro call: (1) Merlin Intake MS Teams chatbot (which 'Jagger doesn't even have', per Zycus rep) for end-user adoption, and (2) Agentic AI capabilities (touchless invoices, tail-spend negotiation, supplier onboarding) that differentiate Zycus from Jaggaer's more traditional workflow-based platform. Wendy showed interest in both. The current threat level is LOW because Wendy is in solo discovery mode, not running a live RFP or multi-vendor eval yet; however, the 12–18 month window leaves time for other vendors (Coupa, Ariba, GEP) to enter her shortlist. Zycus's strategic play is to stay top-of-mind via light nurture touches and to position the AI narrative as the primary differentiator when Wendy re-engages in late 2026 or early 2027.

competitors.name: Jaggaer
competitors.quote: We are currently utilizing a tool right now. We're still under contract for, like, another year and a half. So right now is the time that I really wanna start just exploring what else is out there.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin Intake (MS Teams chatbot for end-user adoption, which Jaggaer lacks) and Agentic AI (touchless invoices, tail-spend negotiation, supplier onboarding automation); position these as the leap-forward capabilities that solve Wendy's stated pain (maverick spend, low adoption, complexity) and justify replacing an incumbent mid-contract. Make the AI story the centerpiece of every future demo and workshop.

ai_positioning_strength.score: 7/10 (strong conceptual positioning, but not yet converted to use-case proof or quantified ROI)
ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI differentiation (Merlin Intake, Agentic AI for tail-spend negotiation and touchless invoices) was clearly articulated on the intro call and resonated with Wendy's stated pain (low adoption, maverick spend). Zycus positioned Merlin Intake MS Teams chatbot as a capability 'Jagger doesn't even have' and demonstrated Agentic AI capabilities (touchless invoices, tail-spend negotiation, supplier onboarding). Wendy showed interest in both and asked qualifying questions. However, the AI narrative was presented at a high level (feature overview, not use-case proof or quantified ROI). When Wendy re-engages for a deeper demo in late 2026 or early 2027, the next step is to deliver a LIVE Merlin Intake use-case demo tailored to her end-user adoption problem and to quantify the tail-spend savings the Agentic negotiation agent can deliver. AI positioning is strong conceptually but must convert to proof and ROI to close.

items.quote: End users bypass system causing maverick spend and loss of contracted pricing. 90% of rollouts fail due to low user adoption or complicated processes.
items.source: Avoma intro call 2026-03-06, Wendy Rocco and Zycus rep (Avoma notes)
items.criterion: End-user adoption and ease of use (reducing complexity and maverick spend)

items.quote: Do you use MS Teams, Wendy? → Yeah. We use it for calls. We use it for meetings. We use it for chat. [Zycus rep demonstrated Merlin Intake chatbot in MS Teams]
items.criterion: MS Teams integration for end-user requests (Merlin Intake chatbot)

items.quote: Are they ad hoc, or do they all come together, or is there a way to right size the need, or is it all in one package? [Zycus rep confirmed modular platform design]
items.criterion: Modularity and right-sizing (not forced into a full-suite package)

items.quote: Are you only a high end market solution? Are you a middle market solution? [Zycus rep confirmed mid-to-large enterprise focus, 750M–10B revenue range]

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong alignment on ease-of-use and AI-driven adoption; modularity and mid-market fit confirmed. Wendy's top priorities (end-user adoption, reducing maverick spend, ease of use) map directly to Zycus's Merlin Intake and Agentic AI capabilities. Wendy showed clear interest in the MS Teams chatbot and asked qualifying questions that Zycus answered positively (modularity, mid-market fit, support options). No misalignment surfaced on the intro call. The next fit validation will occur when Wendy requests deeper functional demos and use-case testing in late 2026.

hard.ais_why:
• Wendy Rocco sat through March 2026 demo of Merlin Intake chatbot in Teams, Anna autonomous tail-spend negotiation, and AI-driven supplier onboarding. But she cut Edward off mid-pitch saying 'I really wanna understand more of the functionality' rather than the AI story.
• Her takeaway: 'continue market review over the next 12-18 months' and contact Ed directly if she wants a tailored demo. Jaggaer contract has 18 months left so no urgency.
• Polite curiosity, demo absorbed, but not buying the agentic vision yet - she's exploring the broader market. Score reflects exposure to all AI cues with low conversion signal.
hard.next_step: June 16 2026 - Wendy: Continue market review over the next 12–18 months and reach out to Edward directly if you’d like to explore a deeper discussion or tailored demo

gaps: Q2 SOQL (authoritative direct custom fields): AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c are null/unknown — no AI excitement score on record from Salesforce; inferred AI Curious tier from call evidence only.

Q2 SOQL: Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c are null/unknown — no stage-date series for true time-in-stage math; using CreatedDate and stage only.

Q3 SOQL (synthesis-source custom fields): many synthesis-map fields not read this sweep due to INVALID_FIELD or not queried (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, Business_Requirements__c, etc.); this limits MEDDPICC synthesis from Salesforce fields. However, the intro call on 2026-03-06 provided substantive MEDDPICC evidence (pain, competition, decision criteria, champion), so this is a hygiene gap (field-read failure), not a knowledge gap (the insight exists in the call).

Only 1 transcribed buyer call (intro call 2026-03-06); no follow-up calls, no demos, no RFI/RFP discussions, no multi-stakeholder meetings. This limits visibility into decision process, EB/DM engagement, and competitive shortlist evolution. Confidence set to Medium due to thin call coverage (1 substantive call) and long time since last buyer engagement (112 days).

No field history movement on Stage, Amount, CloseDate, or ForecastCategory since creation — the opp has been static in Qualified/Pipeline since 2026-03-09. This is consistent with the buyer's early-stage timeline, not a data-integrity issue, but limits forward-slip and momentum analysis.
